= .github Directory
:description: GitHub-specific configuration and automation for paint.type

// NOTE: The project is named "paint.type" but the GitHub repository is "paint-type"

This directory contains all GitHub-specific configuration files, workflows,
templates, and policies for the paint-type repository.

== Purpose

The `.github/` directory serves as the integration point between the paint-type
project and GitHub's platform features, including:

* Continuous Integration / Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) workflows
* Issue and discussion templates
* Security policies and procedures
* Community governance and conduct standards
* Automated dependency management
* Repository settings and automation

== Contents by Functional Grouping

=== Configuration Files

* `CODEOWNERS`:: Defines repository ownership and code review requirements. Maps files/directories to owners and required reviewers.
* `FUNDING.yml`:: Configures GitHub Sponsors funding for the project.
* `dependabot.yml`:: Configures automated dependency updates and security patch management.
* `settings.yml`:: Repository-level settings and configurations.

=== Governance and Community

* `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`:: GitHub-required file. Defines expected behavior and community standards. link:CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md[View].
* `CONTRIBUTING.md`:: GitHub-required file. Guidelines for contributing to the project. link:CONTRIBUTING.md[View].
* `GOVERNANCE.adoc`:: Project governance model (BDFL). Defines roles, decision-making, and maintainership. link:GOVERNANCE.adoc[View].
* `MAINTAINERS`:: List of project maintainers with contact information.
* `SUPPORT`:: Support channels and contact information.

=== AI and Automation

* `copilot-instructions.adoc`:: Instructions for AI assistants and Copilot. link:copilot-instructions.adoc[View].
* `copilot-instructions.md`:: Legacy markdown version (maintained for compatibility).

=== Security

* `SECURITY.md`:: GitHub-required file. Security policy, vulnerability reporting, and disclosure procedures. link:SECURITY.md[View].

=== Templates

* `pull_request_template.md`:: Template for pull request descriptions. Ensures consistent PR formatting.

=== Subdirectories

* `workflows/`:: GitHub Actions workflow files for CI/CD automation.
* `ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`:: Templates for different issue types (bug, feature, documentation, etc.).
* `DISCUSSION_TEMPLATE/`:: Templates for GitHub discussion categories.

== Purpose of Each Component

=== CODEOWNERS

Ensures that changes to critical parts of the codebase are reviewed by appropriate
maintainers. Prevents unintended modifications to sensitive areas without proper
oversight.

=== Workflows (workflows/)

Automated CI/CD pipelines that:

* Run tests on pull requests and pushes
* Enforce code quality standards
* Build and deploy artifacts
* Run security scans
* Verify compliance with project standards

See link:workflows/README.adoc[workflows/README.adoc] for details.

=== Issue Templates (ISSUE_TEMPLATE/)

Predefined templates for different issue types that:

* Guide contributors to provide necessary information
* Standardize issue reporting
* Improve triage efficiency
* Reduce back-and-forth for common questions

=== Discussion Templates (DISCUSSION_TEMPLATE/)

Templates for GitHub discussions that:

* Structure community conversations
* Provide context for different discussion topics
* Guide users to appropriate channels

== AI Manifest Structure

This directory follows the hierarchical AI manifest structure:

* `0-AI-MANIFEST.a2ml` (repo root):: Root AI manifest for paint-type
* `0.1-AI-MANIFEST.a2ml` (this directory):: Level 1 manifest for `.github/`
* `0.2-AI-MANIFEST.a2ml` (subdirectories):: Level 2 manifests for each subdirectory

Each manifest provides machine-readable metadata about the directory's purpose,
contents, and authority structure.
